sentences.en: The Industrial Revolution began in Britain in the mid-1700s and by the 1830s and 1840s had spread to many other parts of the world, including the United States.
sentences.zh: 工业革命于18世纪中叶始于英国，到了19世纪三四十年代已经传播到世界许多其他地区，包括美国。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + 现在分词举例
grammar.note: 主语 The Industrial Revolution 后接由 and 连接的两个并列谓语：began in Britain 与 had spread to...；by the 1830s and 1840s 为时间状语，配合过去完成时 had spread 表示到那时之前已完成的动作；including the United States 为现在分词短语举例。

sentences.en: In Britain, it was a period when a largely rural, agrarian* society was transformed into an industrialised, urban one.
sentences.zh: 在英国，那是一个以农村和农业为主的社会转变为工业化城市社会的时期。

grammar.type: when 定语从句 + 被动语态
grammar.note: 主干是 it was a period；when 引导定语从句修饰 period，从句主干为被动结构 a...society was transformed into...one，one 指代 society；largely rural, agrarian 与 industrialised, urban 分别为两组并列形容词。

sentences.en: Goods that had once been crafted by hand started to be produced in mass quantities by machines in factories, thanks to the invention of steam power and the introduction of new machines and manufacturing techniques in textiles, iron-making and other industries.
sentences.zh: 多亏了蒸汽动力的发明，以及纺织、炼铁等行业中新机器和新制造工艺的引入，曾经靠手工制作的商品开始在工厂里由机器大批量生产。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 被动语态 + thanks to 状语
grammar.note: 主干是 Goods...started to be produced...；that had once been crafted by hand 为定语从句修饰 Goods，用过去完成时被动；thanks to... 为原因状语，其后 the invention of... 与 the introduction of... 为并列宾语。

sentences.en: The foundations of the Industrial Revolution date back to the early 1700s, when the English inventor Thomas Newcomen designed the first modern steam engine.
sentences.zh: 工业革命的基础可以追溯到18世纪初，当时英国发明家托马斯·纽科门设计出了第一台现代蒸汽机。

grammar.type: date back to + when 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 The foundations...date back to the early 1700s，date back to 表“追溯到”；when 引导非限制性定语从句修饰时间 the early 1700s；the English inventor 为 Thomas Newcomen 的前置同位语。

sentences.en: Called the 'atmospheric steam engine', Newcomen's invention was originally used to power machines that pumped water out of mines.
sentences.zh: 纽科门的这项发明被称为“大气式蒸汽机”，最初被用来驱动把水抽出矿井的机器。

grammar.type: 过去分词短语作状语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: 句首 Called the 'atmospheric steam engine' 为过去分词短语作状语，说明主语的称谓；主干是 Newcomen's invention was...used to power machines，为被动语态；that pumped water out of mines 为定语从句修饰 machines。

sentences.en: In the 1760s, the Scottish engineer James Watt started to adapt one of Newcomen's models, and succeeded in making it far more efficient.
sentences.zh: 18世纪60年代，苏格兰工程师詹姆斯·瓦特开始改进纽科门的一款机型，并成功地使其效率大幅提升。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + 动名词宾语
grammar.note: 主语 James Watt 后接由 and 连接的两个并列谓语 started to adapt... 与 succeeded in making...；succeed in doing 表“成功做某事”；making it far more efficient 中 far 修饰比较级 more efficient 表“……得多”。

sentences.en: Watt later worked with the English manufacturer Matthew Boulton to invent a new steam engine driven by both the forward and backward strokes of the piston, while the gear mechanism it was connected to produced rotary motion.
sentences.zh: 瓦特后来与英国制造商马修·博尔顿合作，发明了一种新型蒸汽机，它由活塞的往复冲程共同驱动，而它所连接的齿轮机构则产生旋转运动。

grammar.type: 过去分词后置定语 + while 对比状语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 Watt...worked with...to invent a new steam engine，to invent 表目的；driven by...the piston 为过去分词短语修饰 engine；while 引导对比状语从句，其中 it was connected to 为省略关系词的定语从句修饰 the gear mechanism。

sentences.en: It was a key innovation that would allow steam power to spread across British industries.
sentences.zh: 这是一项关键的创新，使蒸汽动力得以在英国各行业中普及。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 It was a key innovation；that would allow steam power to spread... 为定语从句修饰 innovation，allow sb/sth to do 表“使……能够”；would 表示从过去看的将来。

sentences.en: The demand for coal, which was a relatively cheap energy source, grew rapidly during the Industrial Revolution, as it was needed to run not only the factories used to produce manufactured goods, but also steam-powered transportation.
sentences.zh: 煤炭是一种相对廉价的能源，对它的需求在工业革命期间迅速增长，因为它不仅用于运转生产制成品的工厂，还用于蒸汽动力的交通运输。

grammar.type: 非限制性定语从句 + as 原因状语从句 + not only...but also
grammar.note: 主干是 The demand for coal...grew rapidly；which was a relatively cheap energy source 为插入的非限制性定语从句修饰 coal；as 引导原因状语从句 it was needed to run...；not only...but also 连接两个并列宾语 the factories 与 steam-powered transportation，used to produce... 为过去分词短语修饰 factories。

sentences.en: In the early 1800s, the English engineer Richard Trevithick built a steam-powered locomotive, and by 1830 goods and passengers were being transported between the industrial centres of Manchester and Liverpool.
sentences.zh: 19世纪初，英国工程师理查德·特里维西克造出了一台蒸汽机车；到1830年，货物和乘客已经在曼彻斯特和利物浦这两个工业中心之间往来运输。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 过去进行时被动
grammar.note: and 连接两个分句。前句主干 Richard Trevithick built a steam-powered locomotive；后句主干 goods and passengers were being transported，为过去进行时的被动语态，表当时正在进行；the industrial centres of Manchester and Liverpool 中 of 短语说明这两个中心。

sentences.en: In addition, steam-powered boats and ships were widely used to carry goods along Britain's canals as well as across the Atlantic.
sentences.zh: 此外，蒸汽动力的船只被广泛用于沿英国的运河以及横跨大西洋运送货物。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + as well as 并列
grammar.note: 主干是 steam-powered boats and ships were widely used to carry goods，为被动语态；along Britain's canals 与 across the Atlantic 由 as well as 并列，作 carry 的地点状语；In addition 为承接上文的过渡语。

sentences.en: Britain had produced textiles like wool, linen and cotton, for hundreds of years, but prior to the Industrial Revolution, the British textile business was a true 'cottage industry', with the work performed in small workshops or even homes by individual spinners, weavers and dyers.
sentences.zh: 英国生产羊毛、亚麻和棉花等纺织品已有数百年历史，但在工业革命之前，英国的纺织业是名副其实的“家庭手工业”，其工作由个体的纺工、织工和染工在小作坊甚至家中完成。

grammar.type: 过去完成时 + but 转折 + with 独立主格
grammar.note: but 连接两个分句。前句 Britain had produced textiles... 用过去完成时表更早的历史；后句主干 the British textile business was a true 'cottage industry'；with the work performed... 为“with + 名词 + 过去分词”独立主格结构，说明伴随情况。

sentences.en: Starting in the mid-1700s, innovations like the spinning jenny and the power loom made weaving cloth and spinning yarn and thread much easier.
sentences.zh: 从18世纪中叶开始，珍妮纺纱机和动力织机等创新使得织布以及纺纱线和纺线变得容易多了。

grammar.type: 现在分词状语 + make + 宾语 + 宾补
grammar.note: 句首 Starting in the mid-1700s 为现在分词短语作时间状语；主干是 innovations...made...much easier，为 make + 宾语 + 形容词宾补结构；宾语是并列动名词 weaving cloth 与 spinning yarn and thread；much 修饰比较级 easier。

sentences.en: With these machines, relatively little labour was required to produce cloth, and the new, mechanised textile factories that opened around the country were quickly able to meet customer demand for cloth both at home and abroad.
sentences.zh: 有了这些机器，生产布料所需的劳动力相对较少，而在全国各地开办的新型机械化纺织厂很快就能满足国内外顾客对布料的需求。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 并列句 + 定语从句
grammar.note: and 连接两个分句。前句主干 relatively little labour was required to produce cloth，为被动语态；后句主干 the...factories were quickly able to meet customer demand；that opened around the country 为定语从句修饰 factories；both at home and abroad 表“国内外”。

sentences.en: The British iron industry also underwent major change as it adopted new innovations.
sentences.zh: 随着采用新的革新技术，英国的钢铁业也经历了重大变革。

grammar.type: as 状语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 The British iron industry...underwent major change，underwent 为 undergo 的过去式，表“经历”；as 引导时间/原因状语从句 it adopted new innovations，it 指代 the British iron industry。

sentences.en: Chief among the new techniques was the smelting of iron ore with coke (a material made by heating coal) instead of the traditional charcoal.
sentences.zh: 新技术中最主要的是用焦炭（一种通过加热煤炭制成的材料）而非传统的木炭来冶炼铁矿石。

grammar.type: 表语前置的完全倒装 + 括号同位语
grammar.note: 本句为完全倒装，正常语序是 the smelting of iron ore with coke...was chief among the new techniques；表语 Chief among the new techniques 提前，主语是 the smelting...；括号内 a material made by heating coal 为 coke 的同位解释；instead of 表“而不是”。

sentences.en: This method was cheaper and produced metals that were of a higher quality, enabling Britain's iron and steel production to expand in response to demand created by the Napoleonic Wars (1803–15) and the expansion of the railways from the 1830s.
sentences.zh: 这种方法更便宜，而且能生产出质量更高的金属，从而使英国的钢铁产量得以扩大，以应对拿破仑战争（1803—15）以及19世纪30年代起铁路扩张所带来的需求。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + 定语从句 + 现在分词结果状语
grammar.note: 主干为并列谓语 This method was cheaper and produced metals；that were of a higher quality 为定语从句修饰 metals；enabling...to expand 为现在分词短语表结果，enable sb/sth to do 表“使……能够”；created by... 为过去分词短语修饰 demand。

sentences.en: The latter part of the Industrial Revolution also saw key advances in communication methods, as people increasingly saw the need to communicate efficiently over long distances.
sentences.zh: 工业革命的后期还见证了通信方式的重大进步，因为人们越来越意识到需要在远距离上进行高效沟通。

grammar.type: 拟人化主语 saw + as 原因状语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 The latter part...saw key advances，用 saw（see）拟人化表“出现、见证”；as 引导原因状语从句 people increasingly saw the need to communicate...，此处 saw 意为“意识到”；over long distances 为方式/范围状语。

sentences.en: In 1837, British inventors William Cooke and Charles Wheatstone patented the first commercial telegraphy system.
sentences.zh: 1837年，英国发明家威廉·库克和查尔斯·惠斯通为第一套商用电报系统申请了专利。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + 前置同位语
grammar.note: 主干是 William Cooke and Charles Wheatstone patented the first commercial telegraphy system；British inventors 为两位人名的前置同位语；In 1837 为时间状语。

sentences.en: In the 1830s and 1840s, Samuel Morse and other inventors worked on their own versions in the United States.
sentences.zh: 在19世纪三四十年代，塞缪尔·莫尔斯和其他发明家在美国研制着他们各自的版本。

grammar.type: 主谓 + work on 短语
grammar.note: 主干是 Samuel Morse and other inventors worked on their own versions，work on 表“致力于、研制”；In the 1830s and 1840s 为时间状语，in the United States 为地点状语。

sentences.en: Cooke and Wheatstone's system was soon used for railway signalling in the UK.
sentences.zh: 库克和惠斯通的系统很快就被用于英国的铁路信号传输。

grammar.type: 被动语态
grammar.note: 主干是 Cooke and Wheatstone's system was soon used for railway signalling，为被动语态；for railway signalling 说明用途；in the UK 为地点状语。

sentences.en: As the speed of the new locomotives increased, it was essential to have a fast and effective means of avoiding collisions.
sentences.zh: 随着新型机车速度的提高，拥有一种快速有效的避免碰撞的手段变得至关重要。

grammar.type: as 时间状语从句 + 形式主语
grammar.note: As 引导时间状语从句 the speed...increased；主句用 it 作形式主语，真正主语为 to have a fast and effective means...；of avoiding collisions 为介词短语作后置定语修饰 means。

words.def: 必不可少的；至关重要的

sentences.en: The impact of the Industrial Revolution on people's lives was immense.
sentences.zh: 工业革命对人们生活的影响是巨大的。

grammar.type: 主系表
grammar.note: 主干是 The impact...was immense；of the Industrial Revolution 与 on people's lives 均为介词短语作后置定语修饰 impact，分别说明“谁的影响”和“对什么的影响”。

sentences.en: Although many people in Britain had begun moving to the cities from rural areas before the Industrial Revolution, this accelerated dramatically with industrialisation, as the rise of large factories turned smaller towns into major cities in just a few decades.
sentences.zh: 尽管早在工业革命之前，英国就已有许多人开始从农村迁往城市，但这一进程随着工业化而急剧加速，因为大型工厂的兴起在短短几十年间就把较小的城镇变成了大城市。

grammar.type: Although 让步状语从句 + as 原因状语从句
grammar.note: Although 引导让步状语从句 many people...had begun moving...，用过去完成时；主句是 this accelerated dramatically with industrialisation，this 指代前述迁移现象；as 引导原因状语从句 the rise...turned smaller towns into major cities，turn A into B 表“把A变成B”。

sentences.en: This rapid urbanisation brought significant challenges, as overcrowded cities suffered from pollution and inadequate sanitation.
sentences.zh: 这种快速的城市化带来了严峻的挑战，因为过度拥挤的城市饱受污染和卫生设施不足之苦。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + as 原因状语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 This rapid urbanisation brought significant challenges；as 引导原因状语从句 overcrowded cities suffered from...，suffer from 表“遭受、饱受”；pollution 与 inadequate sanitation 为并列宾语。

sentences.en: Although industrialisation increased the country's economic output overall and improved the standard of living for the middle and upper classes, many poor people continued to struggle.
sentences.zh: 尽管工业化总体上提高了国家的经济产出，并改善了中上层阶级的生活水平，但许多穷人仍然生活艰难。

grammar.type: Although 让步状语从句 + 并列谓语
grammar.note: Although 引导让步状语从句，其内为并列谓语 increased the country's economic output 与 improved the standard of living；主句是 many poor people continued to struggle；overall 作状语表“总体上”。

sentences.en: Factory workers had to work long hours in dangerous conditions for extremely low wages.
sentences.zh: 工厂工人不得不在危险的环境中长时间工作，却只能得到极低的工资。

grammar.type: 主谓 + 多重状语
grammar.note: 主干是 Factory workers had to work long hours，had to do 表“不得不”；in dangerous conditions 为地点/方式状语，for extremely low wages 为目的/条件状语，说明报酬极低；extremely 修饰 low。

sentences.en: These conditions along with the rapid pace of change fuelled opposition to industrialisation.
sentences.zh: 这些状况连同变革的迅猛步伐，加剧了对工业化的反对。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + along with 附加成分
grammar.note: 主干是 These conditions...fuelled opposition；along with the rapid pace of change 为附加成分，不影响谓语单复数（仍以 These conditions 为主）；fuel 此处作动词表“助长、加剧”；to industrialisation 修饰 opposition。

sentences.en: A group of British workers who became known as 'Luddites' were British weavers and textile workers who objected to the increased use of mechanised looms and knitting frames.
sentences.zh: 一群后来被称为“卢德分子”的英国工人，是一些反对越来越多地使用机械化织布机和针织机的英国织工和纺织工人。

grammar.type: 双重定语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 A group of British workers...were British weavers and textile workers；第一个 who became known as 'Luddites' 修饰主语 workers；第二个 who objected to... 修饰表语 weavers and textile workers；object to 表“反对”。

sentences.en: Many had spent years learning their craft, and they feared that unskilled machine operators were robbing them of their livelihood.
sentences.zh: 许多人曾花费多年时间学习自己的手艺，他们担心不熟练的机器操作工正在夺走他们的生计。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: and 连接两个分句。前句 Many had spent years learning their craft，spend time doing 表“花时间做某事”，用过去完成时；后句主干 they feared that...，that 引导宾语从句，rob sb of sth 表“剥夺某人的某物”。

sentences.en: A few desperate weavers began breaking into factories and smashing textile machines.
sentences.zh: 一些走投无路的织工开始闯入工厂，砸毁纺织机器。

grammar.type: 主谓 + 并列动名词宾语
grammar.note: 主干是 A few desperate weavers began...，begin doing 表“开始做”；breaking into factories 与 smashing textile machines 为由 and 连接的并列动名词宾语；break into 表“闯入”。

sentences.en: They called themselves Luddites after Ned Ludd, a young apprentice who was rumoured to have wrecked a textile machine in 1779.
sentences.zh: 他们以内德·卢德的名字自称卢德分子；内德·卢德是一名年轻学徒，据传曾在1779年毁坏了一台纺织机器。

grammar.type: call sb after + 同位语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 They called themselves Luddites，call...after... 表“以……命名”；Ned Ludd 后接同位语 a young apprentice；who was rumoured to have wrecked... 为定语从句，be rumoured to have done 表“据传曾做过”。

sentences.en: The first major instances of machine breaking took place in 1811 in the city of Nottingham, and the practice soon spread across the country.
sentences.zh: 捣毁机器的首批重大事件于1811年发生在诺丁汉市，这种做法很快就蔓延到全国。

grammar.type: 并列句
grammar.note: and 连接两个分句。前句主干 The first major instances...took place，take place 表“发生”；of machine breaking 修饰 instances；后句主干 the practice soon spread across the country，the practice 指代前述捣毁机器的行为。

sentences.en: Machine-breaking Luddites attacked and burned factories, and in some cases they even exchanged gunfire with company guards and soldiers.
sentences.zh: 捣毁机器的卢德分子袭击并烧毁工厂，在某些情况下他们甚至与公司卫兵和士兵交火。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 并列谓语
grammar.note: and 连接两个分句。前句主干为并列谓语 Machine-breaking Luddites attacked and burned factories；后句主干 they even exchanged gunfire with...，exchange gunfire with 表“与……交火”；in some cases 为状语。

sentences.en: The workers wanted employers to stop installing new machinery, but the British government responded to the uprisings by making machine-breaking punishable by death.
sentences.zh: 工人们希望雇主停止安装新机器，但英国政府对这些起义的回应是把捣毁机器定为死罪。

grammar.type: but 转折 + by doing 方式状语
grammar.note: but 连接两个分句。前句主干 The workers wanted employers to stop installing...，want sb to do、stop doing 各为固定结构；后句主干 the British government responded to the uprisings by making machine-breaking punishable by death，by doing 表方式，make + 宾语 + 形容词宾补。

sentences.en: The unrest finally reached its peak in April 1812, when a few Luddites were shot during an attack on a mill near Huddersfield.
sentences.zh: 这场动乱最终在1812年4月达到顶峰，当时几名卢德分子在袭击哈德斯菲尔德附近一家工厂时被枪杀。

grammar.type: 主谓宾 + when 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干是 The unrest finally reached its peak，reach one's peak 表“达到顶峰”；when 引导非限制性定语从句修饰 April 1812，从句用被动 a few Luddites were shot；during an attack on a mill 为时间状语。

sentences.en: In the days that followed, other Luddites were arrested, and dozens were hanged or transported to Australia.
sentences.zh: 在随后的日子里，其他卢德分子被逮捕，数十人被绞死或流放到澳大利亚。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 被动语态
grammar.note: and 连接两个分句，均为被动语态：other Luddites were arrested 与 dozens were hanged or transported to Australia；In the days that followed 为时间状语，that followed 为定语从句修饰 the days。

sentences.en: By 1813, the Luddite resistance had all but vanished.
sentences.zh: 到1813年，卢德分子的抵抗几乎销声匿迹。

grammar.type: 过去完成时 + 习语 all but
grammar.note: 主干是 the Luddite resistance had...vanished，用过去完成时表到1813年之前已完成；all but 为习语，意为“几乎、差不多”，修饰 vanished；By 1813 为时间状语。

questions.title: Questions 1-7 · Note completion
instructions: Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: Steam power — In Watt and Boulton's steam engine, the movement of the 1 ____ was linked to a gear system.
Steam power — A greater supply of 2 ____ was required to power steam engines.
Textile industry — Before the Industrial Revolution, spinners and weavers worked at home and in 3 ____ .
Textile industry — Not as much 4 ____ was needed to produce cloth once the spinning jenny and power loom were invented.
Iron industry — Smelting of iron ore with coke resulted in material that was better 5 ____ .
Iron industry — Demand for iron increased with the growth of the 6 ____ .
Urbanisation — The new cities were dirty, crowded and lacked sufficient 7 ____ .

questions.title: Questions 8-13 · TRUE / FALSE / NOT GIVEN
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 8-13 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

items.prompt: Britain's canal network grew rapidly so that more goods could be transported around the country.
Costs in the iron industry rose when the technique of smelting iron ore with coke was introduced.
Samuel Morse's communication system was more reliable than that developed by William Cooke and Charles Wheatstone.
The economic benefits of industrialisation were limited to certain sectors of society.
Some skilled weavers believed that the introduction of the new textile machines would lead to job losses.
There was some sympathy among local people for the Luddites who were arrested near Huddersfield.

phrases.def: 卢德分子（反对机械化的工人）
